g.data-narration: Welcome to Data Processing Instructions in 8051 Microcontroller. This module covers essential operations for arithmetic, logical, and data manipulation.
Data processing instructions enable the CPU to perform arithmetic, logical, and data manipulation operations essential for embedded systems programming.
g.text: Data processing instructions form the backbone of 8051 programming, enabling the CPU to
perform arithmetic, logical, and data manipulation operations on 8-bit data in registers
and memory locations. These instructions determine code density and execution speed.

g.data-narration: The 8051 instruction set is categorized into four main groups: Arithmetic, Logical, Data Transfer, and Boolean Manipulation instructions.
ADD instruction adds register content to accumulator, setting carry flag if overflow occurs. ADDC includes the carry flag in addition for multi-byte operations.
g.text: A = A + Rn (R0-R7), C=1 if carry from bit7
A = A + Rn + C, for multi-byte addition

g.data-narration: SUBB subtracts register and carry from accumulator. INC and DEC add or subtract 1 without affecting the carry flag, unlike other arithmetic operations.
g.text: A = A - Rn - C, C=1 if borrow

g.data-narration: MUL multiplies A by B (result: A=low byte, B=high byte). DIV divides A by B (A=quotient, B=remainder). DA adjusts BCD addition results.
Logical instructions perform bitwise operations: ANL clears bits, ORL sets bits, XRL complements bits. These are essential for masking operations.
CLR clears accumulator to zero. CPL complements all bits. SWAP exchanges upper and lower nibbles - useful for BCD digit manipulation.
Rotate instructions shift bits: RL/RR rotate without carry, RLC/RRC use carry as part of rotation. No flags affected except through carry.
MOV instructions copy data between accumulator, registers, and memory locations using various addressing modes: register, direct, indirect, and immediate.
MOVX accesses external 64KB RAM. XCH swaps accumulator with operand. XCHD swaps only nibbles. PUSH/POP manage the stack for saving registers.
g.text: Data Transfer: MOVX, XCH, Stack Operations

g.data-narration: Boolean instructions manipulate individual bits in the bit-addressable area (20H-2FH) and SFRs. CLR clears, SETB sets, and CPL complements bits.
g.text: MOV C, Bit / MOV Bit, C

g.data-narration: Bit-level ANL and ORL perform logical operations between carry and a specified bit. Useful for conditional bit operations and status checking.
g.text: Carry = Carry AND (bit OR NOT bit)
Carry = Carry OR (bit OR NOT bit)

g.data-narration: For 16-bit addition: ADD the LSB bytes, then ADDC the MSB bytes to propagate carry. This technique extends to any multi-byte arithmetic.
g.text: Problem: Add Number1 (1234H) at 30H-31H and Number2 (5678H) at 32H-33H, result at 40H-41H
MOV A,30H : ADD A,32H : MOV 40H,A : MOV A,31H : ADDC A,33H : MOV 41H,A

g.data-narration: BCD addition uses DA instruction after ADD/ADDC to convert binary result to valid BCD format. Bit manipulation controls I/O ports efficiently.
g.text: MOV A,#45H : ADD A,#67H : DA A
OR: MOV A,P1 : ORL A,#08H : MOV P1,A

g.data-narration: This comprehensive table summarizes all data processing instructions, their operations, and flag effects for quick reference during exam preparation.
